features.summary: Delta reported record Q4 2024 results with $14.4B revenue (+5.7% YoY) and $1.85 EPS at the top end of guidance. Full-year revenue reached $57B (+4% YoY) with $6.16 EPS and industry-leading 13% ROIC. The company achieved investment-grade status across all three credit agencies and generated $3.4B in free cash flow. Delta announced $1.4B in profit sharing for employees and expects 2025 to be the best financial year in company history with EPS guidance above $7.35 (+20% YoY).
features.management_outlook: Management expects strong momentum continuing into 2025 with Q1 revenue growth of 7-9% and margin expansion of 200+ basis points. Full-year guidance includes EPS >$7.35, free cash flow >$4B, and leverage ratio declining to 2x or less. The company plans 3-4% capacity growth focused 85% in premium cabins and 80% in profitable core hubs. Management highlighted improving industry dynamics with competitors removing unprofitable capacity, creating a more constructive pricing environment.
features.risks: Key risks include potential impact from LA wildfires on demand, though management expects this to be temporary. The company faces ongoing maintenance cost normalization challenges and supply chain constraints in the industry. Competitive capacity additions, particularly in Boston, could pressure yields. International operations remain subject to currency fluctuations and geopolitical uncertainties, though current trends favor US point-of-sale travel to Europe.
features.key_drivers: Premium revenue growth continues to outpace main cabin (+8% YoY) with strong corporate demand (+10% YoY in Q4). American Express co-brand partnership generated $7.4B annually with high single-digit growth expected in 2025. Transatlantic performance exceeded expectations due to favorable USD/EUR exchange rates and extended travel seasons. New partnerships with Uber and YouTube enhance SkyMiles value proposition, while operational excellence maintains industry-leading completion factors and on-time performance.
